headlines.title: MILAM EXPECTED TO RUN FOR CONGRESS
headlines.subtitle: Friends Say Mayor Will Be Formally in Race Before Week Is Out.
headlines.fullText:
Mayor Milam will be a candidate for congress in the July primaries, it is now almost certain. Mayor Milam admitted the matter and many of his friends insist that he will be in the race formally before the week is out. "to enter the race," he said about the blondes." The me urer that the district should have a representative where views are in harmony with the policies of the party and the President.
Has Wide Acquaintance.
Those who are predicting the mayor's candidacy point out that he has a personal acquaintance in the con- tensive than any other. born at Weatherford, Parker county was reared in Hood and Somervell wrest the or the district with the exception of Comanche to Tarrant county his wacuanto practically every voter. He served first as justice of the peace and then as county judge Pour becoming stated with the late Judge Bell in practice. Since becoming mayor, he has been a member of the law firm of Milam, Templeton & Rucker, he ran in the last elective.
No Election Necessary.
Should he enter the congressional race and be elected, there would be an hiatus of but a few weeks in the al term does not begin until March 4 of the same year. No extra election would be necessary to become Mayor. The charter provides if a vacancy occurs in the office of mayor within six months of a general city election, the board of commissioners may, by majority vote of the remaining members, fill the vacancy for the unexpired term.
Grant May Seek Place.
It is generally expected about the city hall that if Milam enters the congressional race, Street Commissioner Grant, former mayor of Fort Worth, will be a candidate for the place. So far as can be learned, it is too far off to make a decision at this time. If that occurs there may be a new line-up. when he was totally unfamiliar with the work for either the police or street department in both of which he had experience. Commissioner Davis likely would be a candidate for the street department for which he ran in the last election.
Now Entries Expected.
City Electrician Crabtree and City Purchasing Agent Graves are expected to be in the race for commissioner and if so, there will be a race on with Charles Harrold. while he was a member of that body. Harrold admits that he is considering the matter. For the police and fire department former Assistant Police Chief Hurdles- ferry and W. C. Thackmon also has announced his intention to become a candidate.

headlines.title: LARGE QUANTITIES OF WAR SUPPLIES RUSHED TO REBELS
headlines.fullText: (CONTINUED FROM PAGE ONE) since the first outbreak of internal hostilities in that republic in November. Instructions went forth to customs agents of the United States government along the border, as well as to the army officers in charge of the border patrol, notifying them of the proclamation by President Wilson, raising the embargo on arms. The border patrol, in so far as it has been preventing the smuggling of arms, will be re- went worth it services. Sure to prevent armed combatants from moving back and forth from one country into the other.

headlines.title: OBSTACLE TO 10-STORY BUILDING IS REMOVED
headlines.subtitle: City Passes Ordinance to Close Alley for New Reynolds Structure.
headlines.fullText: All obstacles in the way of the proposed ten-story building to be erected between Ninth and Tenth on Main streets by W. D. and George T. Reynolds were removed Tuesday when the city commission acted favorably upon the request that the alley back of the property between Main and Commerce streets be closed. The alley already is closed on the other side of Ninth street. At the morning session of the commission the request that the alley be closed was referred to Street Commissioner Grant, City Engineer Von Zuben. The building is to have an elaborate picture show on the ground floor on Main street.

headlines.title: MAIN CLEANING TOO COSTLY, SAYS BLANKE
headlines.subtitle: Sediment in Pipes of Long Standing Only by an expensive process of cleaning out the mains can muddy water be prevented on the south side when a fire in the high parts of the city requires a water pressure that stirs up the sediment in the ends of the mains.
headlines.fullText: "There is sediment in the mains that has been deposited at times in the past. There is no way to get rid of it except by main cleaning. This is done in many places, but it is expensive. The method is to cut an opening in a main. A light wire is attached to a bobbin and floated along to another opening a half mile or more away. Heavier wire is attached to this and pulled through, then a wire cable that fills the main and scrapes the sides and bottom of the main as clean as new. The water made murky by Tuesday was practically clear again Wednesday.
